title: pimelea hirsuta subsp. elliptifolia
intro: Pimelea hirsuta subsp. elliptifolia, also known as hairy rice flower, is a subspecies of the Pimelea hirsuta plant, a shrub native to Western Australia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pimelea hirsuta subsp. elliptifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through various methods, including infusion, decoction, and tincture.
